title: Which Companies Are Hiring Machine Learning Engineers Right Now? (Live ATS Data)
description: 10 companies, 94 live machine learning roles (fetched 2026-07-12) - biggest hirers, 62% remote share, full table.
answer_first: Live careers-board data fetched July 12, 2026 shows 10 companies currently advertising 94 machine learning positions. Reddit tops the table with 27 open roles, and 58 of 94 listings (62%) allow remote work. First capture: 2026-07-12. The initial baseline records 94 live openings to compare future refreshes against. Scroll down for the full role-by-role table: company, title, location, remote status, and posting date.

facts: The 2026-07-12 scrape counted 94 current machine learning openings spanning 10 distinct companies.
Reddit posts 27 of the roles, the most of any company, with Pinterest next at 24.
The remote share sits at 62%: 58 of 94 listings allow remote.
By location, Remote - United States leads with 20 roles.
In the final 7 days before this fetch, 5 of the listed roles were posted.
Initial capture (2026-07-12) logs 94 openings as the comparison baseline.

faq.q: What is the source of these job listings?
faq.a: Each listing was read directly from a company's own public careers board via its ATS API on 2026-07-12. The query 'machine learning' matched 94 open roles across 10 companies, and posting dates are the employers' own.

faq.q: Which employer tops this hiring table?
faq.a: Reddit, with 27 open machine learning roles out of 94 total - ahead of Pinterest at 24. The lead is recomputed from board data at every refresh.

faq.q: Can these jobs be done remotely?
faq.a: Some can: 58 of 94 listings (62%) are marked remote by the employer. Check the Remote column for each specific role.
All rows come from a 2026-07-12 scrape; 5 of them were posted within the preceding 7 days. Because boards change daily, treat the table as a dated snapshot that this URL keeps refreshing.
